findings.description: assemble() loops range(total_chunks - 1) so the final chunk (index N-1) is never concatenated, silently truncating every assembled file (and producing an empty file when total_chunks==1).
dest_path joins UPLOAD_DIR with the unsanitized client-supplied filename, allowing path traversal (e.g. '../../tmp/x.pdf') and arbitrary file write outside the upload directory.
upload_id is interpolated directly into chunk file paths without sanitization, so a crafted upload_id ('../../etc/...') lets chunk writes escape CHUNK_DIR (path traversal / arbitrary write).

receive_chunk writes arbitrary-sized data to disk with no per-chunk or cumulative size limit and no cap on total_chunks, enabling disk-exhaustion DoS (MAX_FILE_MB / file_size_ok are never enforced in the pipeline).

verify_checksum does f.read() loading the entire file (up to 200MB+, effectively unbounded since size is unenforced) into memory instead of streaming in blocks, risking memory exhaustion under concurrency.

The shared self._sessions dict and per-session received set are mutated without any locking, so concurrent start/receive_chunk/assemble/abort calls race (lost updates, double-assembly, inconsistent state).

Extension allow-listing via splitext is the only content check and is weak (e.g. 'evil.php.pdf' passes); combined with the unsanitized filename it provides no real protection over the write destination.
del self._sessions[upload_id] raises KeyError if the session was already removed by a concurrent abort() or a second assemble(), unlike abort() which safely uses pop(..., None).
No file I/O error handling: a missing chunk file, disk-full, or permission error during assembly raises an unhandled exception and leaves a partial/corrupt dest_path on disk with no cleanup.
Two upload sessions sharing the same filename overwrite each other's stored output (last-writer-wins) with no collision detection.
Re-sending an already-received chunk index silently overwrites the prior chunk and is counted only once in the received set, so corrupted/duplicate chunks pass the completeness check undetected.
verify_checksum is fully optional and decoupled from assemble(); nothing enforces that a stored file was ever integrity-checked before use.
data is not validated to be bytes and filename is not checked for emptiness, deferring failures to lower-level open()/write() exceptions.
